CHAPTER 282
(H. B. 444)
AN ACT relating to the payment of certain expenses of county attorneys,
and declaring an emergency to exist.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Each county attorney of the state shall be paid
out of the state treasury, each month, beginning August 1, 1962,
the sum of one hundred and twenty-five dollars, which sum is
hereby declared to be the equivalent of the minimum sum that
each county attorney will expend each month in the performance
of his official duties directed to be performed for the Common
wealth, for postage, telephone service, travel, criminal investiga
tion, supplies, stationery and books for which there are no pro
visions for payment. The Commissioner of Finance shall, on the
first of each month beginning August 1, 1962, draw his warrant on
the Treasurer in favor of each county attorney for the sum stated
in this section, and the same shall be paid from the revolving
fund created by Section 2 of this Act. The sums so received shall
be expended by each county attorney in his discretion, the manner
in which the same shall be applied in payment or reimbursement
of such expenses being determined by such officer.
Section 2. To provide the revenue necessary to carry out
the provisions of this Act, a state docket fee of two dollars and fifty
cents, in addition to fees now provided by law is hereby levied
upon each conviction in each criminal case carrying a fine when
the fine is paid into the State Treasury. The funds raised by such
tax shall be paid into the State Treasury, and shall be deposited
into a revolving fund which hereby is created and designated
the County Attorneys' State Expense Account, and from which
fund the appropriations under this Act shall be disbursed.
Section 3. Any surplus in excess of forty-five thousand
dollars which shall have accumulated to the County Attorneys'
State Expense Account at the close of any fiscal year, subsequent
to the close of the fiscal year 1961-1962, shall be transferred to
and become a part of the General Fund of the Commonwealth

Chapter 283 975
Section 4. Whereas, present economic conditions are such
as to impose an unconscionable burden upon the county attorneys
of the Commonwealth in the performance of the duties directed
to be performed for the Commonwealth, and to create a threat
to the expeditious and efficient administration of law enforcement,
an emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall take effect
upon its passage and approval by the Governor.
Approved March 22, 1962
CHAPTER 283
(H. B. 445)
AN ACT relating to the code of practice in criminal cases.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
( 1 ) A guaranteed arrest bond certificate presented by the
person whose signature appears thereon shall be accepted in lieu
of cash bail in an amount not to exceed two hundred dollars as
a bail bond to guarantee the appearance of such person in any
court of this Commonwealth, at the time required by such court,
when he is arrested for violation of any law of this Commonwealth
or traffic ordinance of any municipality therein relating to the
operation of a motor vehicle. A guaranteed arrest bond certifi
cate so presented as a bail bond is subject to the same forefiture
and enforcement provisions as a bail bond or cash bail, but is
not subject to Sections 76, 77, 78 and 82 of the Code of Practice
in Criminal Cases. However:
(a) The violation must have been committed prior to the
expiration date shown on the guaranteed arrest bond certificate,
and
(b) A guaranteed arrest bond certificate may not be ac
cepted when a person is arrested for violation of subsection (2)
of KRS 189.520 or KRS Chapter 281.
(2) As used in this section, "guaranteed arrest bond certifi
cate" means a printed card or other certificate issued by an
association or insurance company to any of its members or in

976 Acts of the General Assembly
sureds, which is signed by the member or insureds and contains
the printed statement that such association or insurance company
and a surety company licensed to do business in this Common
wealth or an insurance company authorized to transact both auto
mobile liability insurance and surety business.
(a) Guarantee the appearance of the person whose sig
nature appears on the card or certificate, and
( b ) Will, in the event of the failure of such person to appear
in court at the time set for appearance, pay any fine or forfeiture
imposed upon such person in an amount not to exceed two hun
dred dollars.
Approved March 22, 1962
CHAPTER 284
(H. B. 466)
AN ACT relating to common carriers.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
.
Section 1. No taxicab owner or operator shall solicit
passengers who may be standing or waiting along the route or
any part of the route covered by the certificate of a common
carrier outside of the corporate limits of a city or outside the limits
of an unincorporated community, as the case may be. This
provision shall in no way be construed as to prevent any indi
vidual from having free choice of transportation media.
Section 2. Section 281.805 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is repealed.
Approved March 22, 1962
CHAPTER 285
(H. B. 471)
AN ACT relating to costs and expenses of county judges.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:

Chapter 285 977
Section 1. Section 453.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
In a misdemeanor case before a county judge, the judge
shall, if the accused is convicted, tax as costs $5.50. [$4.50] The
judge shall each month pay into the county treasury $4.50 of
each levy of [the] costs so collected, with an itemized statement,
verified by him, showing the persons from whom the costs were
received. The judge shall pay $1.00 of each levy of costs so
collected into the state treasury in a manner prescribed by the
Commissioner of Finance.
Section 2. (1) A revolving fund is established and desig
nated as the County Judge Expense Allowance Fund. The
money paid into the state treasury under KRS 453.030 shall be
credited to this fund. For the purposes of KRS 45.140 the
Commissioner of Finance is head of the budget unit in relation
to this fund.
(2) The purpose of the fund is to provide a source from
which payments may be made to county judges to defray or
partly defray the expenses of maintaining their offices.
(3) Any surplus in the fund at the end of a fiscal year
shall be transferred to the general fund.
Section 3. (1) The Commissioner of Finance shall de
termine the amount that is present in the fund on the last days
of the months of September, December, March, and June. Pay
ments shall be made quarterly to county judges in an amount
determined as provided in this section.
(2) Except as provided in paragraph (b) of subsection
( 3 ) , the maximum expense allowance is $300 per quarter-year.
(3) (a) If the money present in the fund on the day
mentioned in subsection (1) divided by 120 equals an amount
less than $300, then that amount shall be paid.
(b) If the money present in the fund on the day mentioned
in subsection (1) divided by 120 equals an amount as much as
or more than $300, then $300 shall be paid, except that:
1. if the amount is more than $300, and
2. if the total amount previously paid to each judge dur

978 Acts of the General Assembly
ing the fiscal year is less than $300 times the number of previous
payments, then the payment shall be in an amount not larger
than is necessary to make the total amount paid during the fiscal
year equal to $300 times the number of payments.
(4) In no case shall a county judge receive more than
$1,200 in one fiscal year.
Section 4. This Act becomes effective July 1, 1962.
Approved March 22, 1962
CHAPTER 286
(H. B. 492)
AN ACT relating to sewer and sanitation services.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. In the interest of the public health and for the
purpose of providing adequate sanitation and drainage facilities
in each county containing a city of the first class, there may be
created and established therein a sanitation district under the
provisions of this chapter, having the powers, duties and functions
as in this chapter prescribed, and to be known by and under the
name of (Name of county) Sanitation
District. Such a district under its name shall be a public body
corporate and political subdivision, with power to adopt, use and
alter at its pleasure a corporate seal, sue and be sued, contract
and be contracted with, and in other ways to act as a natural
person, within the purview of this chapter. Such district shall
encompass all territory within the county except territory em
braced in a metropolitan sewer district organized under KRS
Chapter 76, a sanitation district organized under KRS Chapter
220 or a city served by its own sewer and drainage system.
Section 2. Whenever by resolution of the fiscal court of such
county and approved by the county judge thereof, it is declared
for the best interests of such county and the inhabitants thereof,
that such district be created under the provisions of this chapter,

Chapter 286 979
the clerk of the fiscal court of such county shall file a certified
copy of such resolution with the county court clerk of the county.
Section 3. ( 1 ) The business, activities and affairs of such
district shall be managed, controlled and conducted by a board
composed of five members, all of whom shall be appointed by
the county judge of such county subject to the approval of the
fiscal court, and which five members thus appointed shall consti
tute the board of such district. Not more than three members
of the board shall be affiliated with the same political party.
(2) Each such member shall be at least twenty-five years
of age and a resident of such county. No officer or employe of
such city or county, whether holding a paid or unpaid position,
shall be eligible for appointment as a member of such board, but
nothing in this section shall be construed to disqualify a member
of such board from serving as a member of the board of a metro
politan sewer district organized pursuant to KRS 76.010, 76.020
and 76.030.
(3) On the first day of July following the creation of the
district by the fiscal court of such county, or as soon thereafter
as may be practical, the county judge, subject to the approval
of the fiscal court, shall appoint one member for a term of two
years, two members for a term of three years, and two members
for a term of four years. Upon the expiration of each of the
terms and thereafter, the term of each member shall be four
years, ending on July first. A member is eligible to succeed him
self, and shall continue in office until his successor has been ap
pointed and qualified. Vacancies in the membership shall be
filled for the unexpired portion of the term by the county judge,
subject to the approval of the fiscal court.
(4) Any member of the board may be removed for cause,
after hearing by the county judge, and after at least ten days'
notice in writing has been given to the member. The notice shall
embrace the charges preferred against him. At the hearing he
may be represented by counsel. The finding of the county judge
shall be final and removal results in vacancy in such office.
( 5 ) Each member of the board shall be paid ten dollars for
each meeting of the board attended by such member, but no
member of the board shall be paid more than two hundred and

980 Acts of the General Assembly
forty dollars during any fiscal year of the board, nor for more
than twenty-four meetings held during any fiscal year of the
board.
(6) The board in accordance with its bylaws shall elect
from its members a chairman and a vice-chairman. It shall em
ploy a secretary-treasurer and such other officers and employes
as it deems requisite for the performance of its duties. The
board shall require the secretary-treasurer and such other officers
and employes as it deems advisable to execute faithful per
formance bonds, in such sums as the board may fix. The prem
iums for such bonds shall be paid by the district.
(7) The board may contract with a metropolitan sewer
district organized under KRS Chapter 76 for the administration
and management of the business, activities and affairs of the sani
tation district.
Section 4. (1) The district created under the provisions
of this chapter may:
(a) Prepare or cause to be prepared and to be thereafter
revised and adopted for sewer and drainage systems, plans, de
signs and estimates of costs, of a system of trunk, intercepting,
connecting, lateral and outlet sewers, storm water drains, pump
ing and ventilating stations, disposal plants, treatment plants and
works, and all other appliances and structures, which in the
judgment of the board will provide an effective and advantageous
means for relieving areas in the county which may be embraced
in the district from inadequate sanitary and storm water drainage
and from inadequate sanitary disposal and treatment of the sewage
thereof, or such sections or parts of such system as the board may
from time to time deem proper or convenient to construct, con
sistent with the plans and purposes of this chapter, and the board
may take all such steps as the board may deem proper and neces
sary to effect the purposes of this subsection.
(b) Construct any additions, betterments and extensions to
the facilities of the district, by contract or contracts, or under,
through, or by means of its own officers, agents, and employes.
However, before any construction of new sewers, or the extension
of any existing sewers is begun, the plans therefor shall be first
approved by the county engineer of such county, except that if

Chapter 286 981
the project is within the corporate limits of any incorporated city,
drainage district or other political subdivision within Jefferson
County, then such prior approval also shall be obtained from the
governing authorities of any such political subdivisions or district.
(c) Establish, construct, operate and maintain, as a part
of the sewer and drainage system of the district, sewage treatment
and disposal plants and systems and all the appurtenances and
appliances thereunto belonging. Such sewage treatment and dis
posal plants may be located as the board may deem expedient.
(d) Acquire and hold such personal property as the board
may deem necessary and proper for carrying out the corporate
purposes of the district and to dispose of personal property when
the district has no further need therefor.
(e) Acquire by purchase, gift, lease or by condemnation,
such real property or any interest, right, easement or privilege
therein, as the board may determine necessary, proper and con
venient for the corporate purposes of the district, and to use the
same so long as such corporate existence shall continue, and same
is necessary or useful for the corporate purposes of the district.
Condemnation proceedings may be instituted in the name of the
district pursuant to a resolution of the board declaring the neces
sity for the taking, and the method of condemnation shall be the
same as provided in KRS 416.120. When the board by resolution
declares that any real property which it has acquired, or any
interest therein, is no longer necessary or useful for the corporate
purposes of the district, such real property and interest therein
may be disposed of.
(f) Make bylaws and agreements for the management and
regulation of its affairs and for the regulation of the use of
property under its control and for the establishment and collection
of sewer rates, rentals and charges.
(g) Make contracts and execute all instruments necessary
or convenient in the premises.
(h) Borrow money and to issue negotiable bonds, and pro
vide for the rights of the holders thereof.
(i) Fix and collect sewer rates, rentals, and other charges,
for service rendered by the facilities of the district.

982 Acts of the General Assembly
(j) Enter on any lands, waters and premises for the pur
pose of making surveys, and soundings and examination.
(k) Approve or revise the plans and designs of all sewer,
storm and drainage systems, their trunk, intercepting, connecting,
lateral and outlet lines or sewers, storm water drains, pumping
and ventilating stations, disposal and treatment plants and works
proposed to be constructed, altered or reconstructed by any other
person or corporation, private or public, in any areas in the county
embraced in the district. No such sewers, drains, pumping or
ventilating stations, or disposal or treatment plants or works shall
be constructed or reconstructed without approval by the board
of the district. Any such work shall be subject to inspection and
supervision of the district.
( 2 ) No enumeration of powers in this chapter shall operate
to restrict the meaning of this general grant of power or to exclude
other powers comprehended within this general grant.
Section 5. (1) Any person or corporation, public or
private, in anv areas in the county embraced in the district sub
mitting for approval plans and designs of sanitary sewers or storm-
water drainage facilities, or any combination thereof, to be con
structed by such other person or corporation, private or public,
pursuant to the provisions of paragraph (k) of subsection (1) of
Section 4 of this Act, shall file with the district a written applica
tion for such approval or other action upon the plans and designs
submitted by such person or corporation.
(2) When the district receives an application for approval
of plans or designs of sanitary sewers or storm-water drainage
facilities, or any combination thereof, to be constructed by some
individual or corporation, the district may examine, inspect and
investigate, as seems to be advisable, the sufficiency of the facili
ties which the application seeks to construct, to serve the purposes
intended, and may establish and make reasonable charges for
such services on the basis of a schedule adjusted according to the
services required to make such investigation or on any other
reasonable method.
( 3 ) When it appears to the district that the construction of
any sanitary sewer or storm-water drainage facilities, or any com
bination thereof, being made by any other individual or corpora

Chapter 286 983
tion requires inspection and supervision in order to assure the pro
tection of public health and the proper subsequent completion of
such facility for the purposes intended, the district shall include
such finding in its order approving, modifying or disapproving
the particular plans and projects, and shall charge such person or
corporation for such inspection and supervision on the basis of
the actual cost of inspection plus a reasonable additional cost of
supervision.
Section 6. (1) The district may establish a schedule of
rates, rentals and charges, to be collected from all the real prop
erty served by the facilities of such district, may prescribe the
manner in which and time at which such rates, rentals and
charges are to be paid, and may change such schedule from time
to time as the district may deem necessary, advisable or ex
pedient. Such schedule may be based upon ( a ) the consumption
of water on premises connected with such facilities, taking into
consideration commercial and industrial use of water; or (b)
the number and kind of plumbing fixtures connected with such
facilities; or (c) the number of persons served by such facilities;
or (d) a determination by the district on any other basis or
classification which the district may determine to be fair and
reasonable, whether similar or dissimilar to those enumerated;
or (e) any combination thereof. This schedule may include
additional charges for treatment of sewage, with a surcharge
where the sewage contains industrial wastes or other wastes in
excess of limitations established by regulations of the district.
(2) Prior to the final adoption or modification of such
schedule, the district shall adopt a proposed schedule and pub
lish notice thereof once a week for three consecutive weeks in
a daily newspaper published in the county having the largest
circulation therein. The notice so published shall be dated as
of the date of first publication thereof and shall state that the
proposed or revised schedule of rates, rentals and charges will
remain open for inspection in the office of the district for thirty
days from the date of such notice, and that objections thereto
in writing may be filed during said period with the district
by any person aggrieved thereby. The district shall examine
and hear any and all such complaints, may modify the proposed
schedule, and shall adopt and establish a final schedule within

984 Acts of the General Assembly
sixty days after the date of such notice. However, the schedule
finally adopted shall be sufficient and adequate to cover the
purposes of this chapter. Such schedule shall be uniform for all
property falling with the same classification, which classifica
tion may be based upon the length of time the property has
been in the district, the drainage area within which the prop
erty lies or any similar or dissimilar reasonable classification.
The schedule so adopted and established shall thereafter be
the rates, rentals and charges for the use of the facilities of the
district, until changed in the manner provided in this chapter.
Such schedule of rates, rentals and charges shall be established
and revised from time to time so as to produce, together with
other sources of revenue authorized by this chapter, aggregate
revenues to the district sufficient (a) for the payment of the
interest on and principal of all revenue bonds and other obliga
tions of the district; ( b ) for the payment of all cost and expenses
of operating and maintaining the sewer and drainage system
of the district, including but not limited to the salaries, wages
and fees of all officers and employes of the district; and (c)
for the payment of all cost of renewals and replacements of
such system. However, all such expenses, salaries, wages and
fees necessary or incident to improvements for the account of
which bonds are issued, may be included as a part of the cost
of such improvements and paid from the proceeds of such bonds.
The district may collect such rates, rentals and charges, or cause
them to be collected and paid to it by such agencies as it may
designate, and with whom it may make such contracts or ar
rangements as the district deems proper. The use of all monies
of the district received from any and all sources is hereby
limited exclusively and shall be devoted solely to the payment
of all obligations of the district and board created by this chapter,
and no funds from any sources authorized by this chapter shall
be diverted to any other purposes than those in this chapter
set forth.
(3) Whenever any such sewer rates, rentals or charges for
services rendered shall remain unpaid for a period of thirty days
after the same shall become due and payable, the district shall
declare such property, the owner thereof, and the user of such
service, delinquent until such time as all service rates, rentals
and charges are fully paid and may cut off the sewer connec

Chapter 286 985
tion and service. It is hereby declared to be unlawful for any
delinquent to use water from any public water service or system
and discharge same into a public sewer. No public water service
or system shall furnish such delinquent with water to be dis
charged into a public sewer. The district may enter into agree
ments with any public water company or service providing for
the discontinuance of water service to such delinquents.
Section 7. ( 1 ) The district shall, as promptly as possible,
rehabilitate, construct, improve and extend any sewer and drain
age system taken over and controlled by
it,which shall include,
but
isnot limited to, disconnection of storm water drains and
constructing outlets therefor, where such work
isnecessary to
relieve existing sanitary sewers of storm water loads, in order
to permit the efficient operation of such sanitary sewers.
(2) The district may make rules and regulations for the
submission of bids and the construction of such additions, better
ments and extensions, or any part thereof. No contract shall be
entered into for construction work or for the purchase of ma
terials, unless the contractor shall give an undertaking with cor
porate surety in an amount approved by the board for the faith
ful performance of the contract. As to contracts entered into for
construction, such undertaking shall provide, among other things,
that the person or corporation entering into such contract with
the district will pay for all materials furnished and services
rendered in the performance of the contract, and that any person
or corporation furnishing such materials or rendering such serv-
vices, may maintain an action thereon to recover for the same
against the obligor in the undertaking and the surety, as though
such person or corporation was named therein.
Section
8.
(
1
) The district may acquire
bypurchase, gift
or eminent domain proceedings, the fee, or such right, title,
interest or easement, in such lands as may be deemed by the
district necessary for any of the purposes mentioned in this
chapter, whether within or without the district and any personal
property necessary for the purposes of the district. Such lands
or interest therein, or personal property may be so acquired
whether or not the same are owned or held for public use
by
corporations, associations, or other persons having the power

986 Acts of the General Assembly
of eminent domain, or otherwise held or used for public pur
poses. But nothing in this chapter shall be construed to confer
upon or recognize in the district any power to acquire by agree
ment, purchase or gift, or by eminent domain proceedings, any

the boundaries of any metropolitan sewer district organized
under KRS Chapter 76 or any sanitation district organized under
KRS Chapter 220, except necessary rights of way for trunk
sewers through such districts. Forthwith upon the acquisition of
any such fee, right, title, interest or easement, or personal prop
erty, the same shall be and become dedicated to the uses and
purposes of the district.
(2) The method of condemnation of such property shall
be (a) as provided in KRS 416.120, (b) as provided in subsection
(6) of KRS 76.080, or (c) as provided in KRS 416.230 to 416.310,
or ( d ) as provided in this chapter.
(3) When the district has filed a proceeding to condemn
land or any interest therein or personal property, pursuant to
KRS 416.230 to 416.310, and the board of the district determines
that the necessity for procuring possession of the property is
urgent, it may pass a resolution, at the time that such condemna
tion is authorized or at any time thereafter, for a declaration of
taking, declaring that such lands are to be taken for the use of
the district. The declaration of taking shall contain, or have
annexed thereto, the following:
(a) A statement of the authority under which and, the
public use for which, the lands are taken;
(b) A description of the lands taken sufficient for identi
fication thereof;
(c) A statement of the estate or interest in the lands taken
for such public use; and
(d) A plat showing the lands taken.
(4) At any time after the report of the commissioners has
been made, the district may file the declaration of taking and
make a deposit with the clerk of the court of the sum of money
stated in the award of the commissioners appointed. Title to
such lands in fee simple or such lesser estate as is specified

Chapter 286 987
in the declaration or to the personal property shall then vest
in the district, and the right to just compensation for such land
or interest therein or personal property shall vest in the persons
entitled thereto. Compensation shall be ascertained and awarded
in such proceeding as otherwise provided in KRS 416.230 to
416.310.
Section 9. (1) Where railroad tracks, street railroad
tracks, gas or water pipes, telephone wires, telegraph wires,
electric light or power lines or conduits, for carrying telephone
or telegraph or electric wires, or poles, or other structures of any
public service corporation, extend in, along or across any part
of the work authorized by this chapter, the person or corpora
tion owning or using such tracks, pipes, wires, conduits, poles,
or other structures, shall:
(a) Make such changes in the same at the expense of the
district as may be required during the construction of any im
provement provided for in this chapter; and
(b) Construct or rebuild at the expense of the district its
tracks, pipes, wires, conduits, poles or other structures, over or
along the work by this chapter authorized and constructed, and
at its own expense maintain the same after such work has been
completed.
(2) However, such changes of that part of such tracks,
pipes, wires, conduits, poles or other structures as may be or
extend in a public way, shall be made at the expense of the public
service corporation owning or using the same only if necessary
to afford the district reasonable use of such public way. Rea
sonable notice in writing of such necessary changes shall be
given by the district to the parties concerned. The district may
enter upon streets, highways, bridges or public places, for the
purpose of constructing any additions, betterments or extensions
of the facilities of the district. Whenever the district has en
tered upon and damaged any street, highway, bridge or other
public place, the same shall be restored to its former condition
at the expense of the district.
Section 10. (1) In order to establish, construct, recon
struct, operate or maintain a comprehensive sewage, storm water

988 Acts of the General Assembly
and surface drainage system within the district, the district,
through its board, may levy an ad valorem tax upon the real
property in the district, not exceeding limits designated by the
Constitution of the Commonwealth. However, no such levv of
the district shall become effective until such levy has been
approved by a vote of the voters pursuant to the approval pro
cedure relating to a tax levy in Sections 11 and 12 of this 1962 Act.
( 2 ) When such tax levy has been fully approved, the county
tax commissioner of the county involved, with the cooperation
of the board, shall note on the tax rolls the taxpayers and valua
tion of the property subject to such tax. The county clerk shall
compute the tax on the regular state and county tax bills in such
manner as may be directed by regulation of the Department of
Revenue.
(3) Such taxes shall be subject to the same delinquency
date, discounts, penalties and interest as are applied to the collec
tion of ad valorem taxes and shall be collected by the sheriff
of the county involved and accounted for to the board. The
sheriff shall be entitled to a fee of one percent of the amount
collected.
Section 11. (1) Whenever the board deems it necessary
to construct, improve, remodel or restore sewage or drainage
facilities, or both, and annual funds raised from other sources
are not sufficient to accomplish such purposes, the board shall
make a careful estimate of the amount of money required for the
purposes and shall by resolution: (a) certify to the proper election
officials that an election for an issue of bonds for such purposes
shall be held; (b) state the amount for which bonds shall be
issued and the purpose to which the proceeds shall be applied;
(c) fix the time the bonds shall run, and if a serial issue, the
amount to mature at each time; (d) limit the rate of interest to
be permitted on the bonds which shall not exceed six percent;
(e) state the total amount of the bonds to be issued; (f) provide
for the levy of a tax to pay the interest and creation of a sinking
fund to retire the bonds at their maturity, and (g) state the
question which shall be submitted to the qualified voters within
such district as to whether such bonds shall be issued for the
purposes and the question as to whether a tax shall be levied to

Chapter 286 989
pay interest and retire the bonds. The question shall be so framed
that the voter may by his vote answer "For" or "Against."
(2) The election shall be held at either the next general
or primary election held not less than sixty days after the resolu
tion of the board described in subsection ( 1 ) of this section has
been made. The election shall be conducted and carried out in
the entire district and in all respects as required by the general
election laws, and shall be held by the same officers as required
by the general election laws.
Section 12. ( 1 ) If a majority of those voting on the ques
tion vote in favor of the proposition, the bonds shall be issued.
They shall be placed under the control of the board and the
board shall determine when and at what price, and how the
bonds shall be sold, the date, number of bonds, denominations,
whether coupon or registered, the rate of interest, the frequency
and place of payment of principal and interest, and other details
as desired, embodied in the bonds or in the resolution providing
for their issue.
(2) The board shall then at once adopt a resolution and
cause the bonds to be at once properly prepared and executed
and otherwise prepared for sale and delivery. The bonds shall
be signed by the chairman and secretary of the board and shall
not be sold for less than par and accrued interest. As the bonds
are sold, their proceeds shall be placed to the credit of the
board in a depository designated by the board and shall be kept
in a separate account. The depository shall be required to
execute proper bond covering the funds.
(3) The board shall levy annually a tax, authorized pur
suant to Section 10 of this 1962 Act, sufficient to raise the sum
for the payment of the interest and to create a sinking fund for
the payment of the bonds at maturity. The bonds shall be a
charge upon the district.
(4) The bonds shall be sold at public sale for such price
or prices as the board shall determine, provided that the interest
costs of the money received from any issue of such bonds shall
not exceed six percent per annum when computed to the maturity
of the bonds according to standard tables of bond values.

990 Acts of the General Assembly
(5) Any resolution or resolutions authorizing any bonds
under this section may contain provisions, which shall be a part
of the contract with the holders of the bonds, as to (a) pledging
all or any part of the gross or net revenues of the district to
secure the payment of the bonds and interest thereon; (b) the
amounts to be raised in each year by rates, rentals and charges,
and the use and disposition thereof, and of any other revenues
of the district; (c) the setting aside of reserves or sinking funds
and the regulation and disposition thereof; (d) limitations on the
right of the district to restrict and regulate the use of its facilities;
(e) limitations on the purposes to which the proceeds of sale of
any issue of bonds then or thereafter to be issued may be applied;
(f) limitations on the issuance of additional bonds; and (g) the
procedure, if any, by which the term of any contract with bond
holders may be amended or abrogated, the amount of bonds the
holders of which must consent thereto and the manner in which
such consent may be given.
(6) The bonds or other obligations of the district shall not
constitute an obligation or indebtedness of the county or of any
city within the county and it shall be plainly stated on the face
of each bond of the district that it has been issued under the
provisions of this chapter, and that it does not constitute an
indebtedness of the county or of any city within the county. All
bonds authorized under this section may be issued without any
other proceedings or happenings of any other conditions or things
than those proceedings, conditions and things which are specified
and required by this chapter.
Section 13. (1) The district shall have power, and it is
hereby authorized from time to time, to issue its negotiable
interest-bearing revenue bonds for any of its corporate purposes,
and it may also, from time to time, issue its negotiable interest-
bearing revenue bonds to refund any of its bonds at maturity
or pursuant to redemption provisions, or at any time before
maturity with the consent of the holders thereof. All such bonds,
including interest thereon, shall be payable solely from and
secured only by revenues of the district realized through the
collection of rates, rentals, or other charges, imposed for use of
the facilities of such district. Such bonds shall be authorized
by resolution of the board and shall bear such date or dates,

Chapter 286 991
mature at such time or times not exceeding forty years from their
respective dates, bear interest at such rate or rates, not exceeding
five percent per annum, payable semi-annually, be in such
denominations and in such form, either coupon or registered,
carry such registration privileges, be executed in such manner, be
payable in such medium of payment at such place or places, and
be subject to such terms of re-redemption, with or without
premiums, as such resolution or resolutions may provide. The
bonds shall be sold at public sale for such price or prices as the
board shall determine, provided that the interest cost of the
money received from any issue of such bonds shall not exceed
five percent per annum when computed to the maturity of the
bonds according to standard tables of bond values.
(2) Any resolution or resolutions authorizing any bonds
hereunder, may contain provisions, which shall be a part of the
contract with the holders of the bonds, as to (a) pledging all
or any part of the gross or net revenues of the district to secure
the payment of the bonds and interest thereon; (b) the amounts
to be raised in each year by rates, rentals, and charges, and the
use and disposition thereof, and of any other revenues of the
district; (c) the setting aside of reserves or sinking funds and
the regulation and disposition thereof; (d) limitations on the
right of the district to restrict and regulate the use of its facilities;
(e) limitations on the purposes to which the proceeds of sale of
any issue of bonds then or thereafter to be issued may be applied;
(f) limitations on the issuance of additional bonds; (g) the pro
cedure, if any, by which the term of any contract with bond
holders may be amended or abrogated, the amount of bonds the
holders of which must consent thereto and the manner in which
such consent may be given.
(3) The bonds or other obligations of the district shall not
constitute an obligation or indebtedness of the county or of any
city within the county and it shall be plainly stated on the face
of each bond of the district that it has been issued under the
provisions of this chapter, and that it does not constitute an
indebtedness of the county or of any city within the county. All
bonds authorized hereunder may be issued without a vote of the
voters and without any other proceedings or happenings of any
other conditions or things than those proceedings, conditions

992 Acts of the General Assembly
and things which are specified and required by this chapter.
Such bonds shall be signed in the name of the district by the
chairman or vice-chairman of the board, attested by the signature
of the secretary-treasurer, with corporate seal of the district
attached.
Section 14. ( 1 ) In the event that the district shall default
in the payment of principal of, or interest on, any of the bonds
issued pursuant to this chapter after the said principal or interest
shall become due, whether at maturity or upon call for redemp
tion, and such default shall continue for a period of thirty days,
or in the event that the district shall fail or refuse to comply with
the provisions of this chapter, or shall default in any agreement
made with the holders of the bonds, the holders of twenty
percent in aggregate principal amount of the bonds then out
standing, by instrument or instruments filed in the office of the
county clerk of the county and proved or acknowledged in the
same manner as a deed to be recorded, may apply to a judge in
the chancery branch of the circuit court of the county, to appoint
a committee to represent all of the bondholders for the purposes
herein provided. Upon such application such judge shall appoint
a trustee and such trustee may, and upon written request of the
holders of twenty percent in principal amount of the bonds of
the district then outstanding shall, in his or its own name, (a) by
mandamus or other suit, action or proceeding at law or in equity,
enforce all rights of the bondholders, including but not limited
to the right to require the district to collect rates, rentals, and
other charges, adequate to carry out any agreement as to, or
pledge of, the revenues of the district, and to require the district
and its officers to carry out any other agreement with the bond
holders and to perform its and their duties under this chapter;
(b) bring suit upon the bonds; (c) by action or suit in equity,
require the district to account as if it were the trustee of an
express trust for the bondholders; (d) by action or suit in equity,
enjoin any acts or things which may be unlawful or in violation
of the rights of bondholders; (e) declare all bonds due and
payable, and if all defaults shall be made good then to annul such
declarations and its consequences.
(2) Any such trustee, whether or not all bonds have been
declared due and payable, shall be entitled as of right, upon

Chapter 286 993
application to the judge in the chancery branch, to the appoint
ment of a receiver, who may enter upon and take possession of
the facilities of the district, or any part or parts thereof, and
operate and maintain the same, and collect and receive all rentals,
rates, and other charges, and other revenues, of the district,
thereafter arising therefrom, in the same manner as the district
and its officers might do, and shall deposit all such monies in a
separate account and apply the same in such manner as such
court shall direct. In any suit, action, or proceeding, by the
trustee, the fees, counsel fees, and expenses of the trustee and
of the receiver, if any, shall constitute disbursements taxable
as costs. All costs and disbursements allowed by the court shall
be a first charge on any revenue derived from the facilities of the
district. Such trustee shall, in addition to the foregoing, have
and possess all of the powers necessary or appropriate for the
exercise of any functions specifically set forth herein or incident
to the general representation of the bondholders in the enforce
ment and protection of their rights.
Section 15. In order to promote and protect its activities
and facilities, and in furtherance of the public health, the district
may enter into contracts with, and thereunder it may permit a
metropolitan sewer district organized under KRS Chapter 76,
sanitation districts organized under KRS Chapter 220, cities,
sewer and drainage districts of whatever nature, located in the
same county, to connect with and use the facilities of the district.
The rates for such service and connections shall be such as may
be agreed upon between the contracting parties.
Section 16. The district is authorized to enter into negotia
tions with the Federal Government and the Commonwealth of
Kentucky, or either of them, or the agencies of both or either,
and apply and obtain from them or either or both of them, any
and all grants-in-aid that may be available.
Section 17. It is hereby found and declared that the crea
tion of the district and the carrying out of its corporate purposes
is in all respects in the interest of the public health and is a
public purpose; that the district is performing a governmental
function in the exercise of the powers conferred upon it by this

Acts of the General Assembly
chapter and it shall be required to pay no taxes or assessments
upon any property owned or acquired by it, under its jurisdiction,
control, possession, or supervision, or upon its activities in the
operation and maintenance of its facilities. Bonds issued by the
district and income therefrom are exempt from taxation.
Section 19. Section 76.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
( 1 ) The business, activities, and affairs of such district shall
be managed, controlled and conducted by a board composed of
five members, three of whom shall be appointed by the
mayor of such city subject to the approval of the city legislative
body, and two of whom shall be appointed by the county judge
of such county subject to the approval of the fiscal court, and
which five members thus appointed shall constitute the board of
such district. Not more than three members of the board shall
be affiliated with the same political party.
(2) Each such member shall be at least twenty-five years
of age; each appointed by the mayor and shall be a resident
of such city and wherein he shall have actually continuously
resided for at least three years next prior to appointment; each
appointed by the county judge shall be a resident of such county
[outside the limits of the city] and wherein he shall have actually
continuously resided for at least three years next prior to appoint
ment. No officer or employe of such city or county, whether
holding a paid or unpaid position, shall be eligible for appoint
ment as a member of such board.
(3) On the first day of July, 1946, or as soon thereafter
as may be practicable, the mayor, subject to the approval of the
city legislative body shall appoint one member for a term of two
years, one member for a term of three years, and one member for
a term of four years, and the county judge, subject to the approval
of the fiscal court, shall appoint one member for a term of three
years and one member for a term of four years, and upon the
expiration of each of said terms respectively and thereafter, the
term of each of such members shall be four years, ending on July
first. A member is eligible to succeed himself, and shall continue
in office until his successor has been appointed and qualified.
Vacancies in the membership shall be filled for the unexpired

Chapter 286 995
portion of the term by the mayor or the county judge as the
case may be, subject to the same approval.
(4) Any member of the board appointed by the mayor may
be removed by the mayor, for cause, after hearing by the mayor,
and after at least ten days' notice in writing shall have been given
to the member, which notice shall embrace the charges preferred
against him. At the hearing he may be represented by counsel.
The finding of the mayor shall be final and removal results in
vacancy in such office. Any member of the board appointed
by the county judge may be removed by the county judge, for
cause, after hearing by the county judge, and after at least ten
days' notice in writing shall have been given to the member, which
notice shall embrace the charges preferred against him. At the
hearing he may be represented by counsel. The finding of the
county judge shall be final and removal results in vacancy in such
office.
(5) The members of the board shall be paid ten dollars
for each meeting of the board attended by such member, but no
member of said board shall be paid more than two hundred and
forty dollars during any fiscal year of the board, nor for more
than twenty-four meetings held during any fiscal year of said
board.
Section 20. Section 76.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
The district created under the provisions of this chapter is
empowered:
(1) To have jurisdiction, control, possession, and super
vision, of the existing sewer and drainage system of such city of
the first class; to maintain, operate, reconstruct, and improve the
same as a comprehensive sewer and drainage system, and to make
additions, betterments, and extensions thereto, within the cor
porate limits of such city of the first class and in areas in the

may be embraced in the district and, until the creation of a sani
tation district pursuant to Sections 1 to 17 of this 1962 Act, and
within the limits of the county containing such city beyond such
city limits, and to have all the rights, privileges, and jurisdiction
necessary or proper for carrying such powers into execution. No

996 Acts of the General Assembly
enumeration of powers in this chapter shall operate to restrict
the meaning of this general grant of power or to exclude other

(2) To prepare or cause to be prepared and to be there

posal and treatment plants and works, and all other appliances

quate sanitary disposal and treatment of the sewage thereof, or

(4 ) To establish, construct, operate, and maintain, as a part

Chapter 286 997
of the sewer and drainage system of the district, sewage treatment
and disposal plants and systems and all the appurtenances and ap
pliances thereunto belonging. Such sewage treatment and dis
posal plants may be located in the city, or beyond the limits of
the city in the county in which the city is located, as the board
may deem expedient.
(5) To acquire and hold such personal property as the
board may deem necessary and proper for carrying out the
corporate purposes of the district and to dispose of personal
property when the district has no further need therefor.
( 6 ) To acquire by purchase, gift, lease, or by condemnation,
such real property or any interest, right, easement, or privilege
therein, as the board may determine necessary, proper and con
venient for the corporate purposes of the district, and to use the
same so long as such corporate existence shall continue, and same
is necessary or useful for the corporate purposes of the district.
Condemnation proceedings may be instituted in the name of the
district pursuant to a resolution of the board declaring the neces
sity for the taking, and the method of condemnation shall be the
same as provided in KRS 416.120. When the board by resolu
tion declares that any real property which it has acquired, or
any interest therein, is no longer necessary or useful for the cor
porate purposes of the district, such real property and interest
therein may be disposed of.
(7) To make bylaws and agreements for the management
and regulation of its affairs and for the regulation of the use of
property under its control and for the establishment and collection
of sewer rates, rentals and charges, which sewer rates, rentals and
charges, applicable within the limits of a city of the first class,
shall be subject to the approval, supervision and control of the
legislative body of such city as hereinafter provided.
(8) To make contracts and execute all instruments necessary
or convenient in the premises.
(9) To borrow money and to issue negotiable bonds and
to provide for the rights of the holders thereof.
(10) To fix and collect sewer rates, rentals, and other
charges, for service rendered by the facilities of the district, which
sewer rates, rentals, and other charges, applicable within the limits

Acti of the General Assembly
of a city of the first class, shall be subject to the approval super
vision and control of the legislative bodv of such citv as herein
after provided.
(11) To enter on any lands, waters and premises for the
purpose of making surveys, and soundings and examinations.
(12) To approve or revise the plans and designs of all
trunk, intercepting, connecting, lateral and outlet sewers, storm
water drains, pumping and ventilating stations, disposal and
treatment plants and works proposed to be constructed, altered

such city]. No such sewers, drains, pumping or ventilating sta
tions, or disposal or treatment plants or works shall be constructed
or reconstructed without approval by the board of the district.
Any such work shall be subject to inspection and supervision
of the district.
Section 21. Section 76.085 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
(1) Any person or corporation, public or private, in any
city of the first class or in areas in the county in which such city
is located outside the city limits which may be embraced in the
district, or, until the creation of a sanitation district pursuant to
Sections 1 to 17 of this 1962 Act, in the remainder of the county
containing such city, [or in the county containing such city]
submitting for approval plans and designs of sanitary sewers
and/or storm-water drainage facilities, to be constructed by such
person or corporation, private or public, pursuant to the pro
visions of subsection (12) of KRS 76.080 shall file with the [a]
metropolitan sewer district a written application for such ap
proval or other action upon the plans and designs submitted by
such person or corporation.
(2) When a metropolitan sewer district receives an appli
cation for approval of plans or designs of sanitary sewers and/or

Chapter 286 999
storm-water drainage facilities to be constructed by some indi
vidual or corporation, the metropolitan sewer district is author
ized and empowered to examine, inspect and investigate, as
seems to be advisable, the sufficiency of the facilities which the
application seeks to construct, to serve the purposes intended,
and to establish and make reasonable charges for such services
on the basis of a schedule adjusted according to the services
required to make such investigation or on any other reasonable
method.
(3) When it appears to the metropolitan sewer district
that the construction of any sanitary sewer and/or storm-water
drainage facility being made by any other individual or cor
poration, requires inspection and supervision in order to assure
the protection of public health and the proper subsequent com
pletion of such facility for the purposes intended, the metro
politan sewer district shall include such finding in its order

Section 22. Section 76.170 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
( 1 ) The initial unit of the district embraces the area that
is coterminous with the boundaries of the city of the first class,
subject to extension in the county beyond the boundaries of such
city, as in this chapter provided. Until a sanitation district is
created, in the county pursuant to Sections 1 to 17 of this 1962
Act, where such a metropolitan district has been formed to in
clude a city of the first class, any territory annexed to such city

system and facilities beyond the corporate limits of such city of
the first class and within the county in which such city is located
whenever the district and the owner or owners of real property
to be served and located outside the limits of such city, by
appropriate written instrument, agree as to apportionment ol
any and all costs of construction work, subsequent maintenance
and operation appertaining thereto, and as to payments by

1000 Acts of the General Assembly
the owner or owners of such real property, of rates, rentals and
charges, for the services and facilities to be thus afforded.
Thereupon such real property served becomes a part of and
is embraced in the district. In like manner the district may
construct and extend its sewer and drainage system and facilities
so as to serve all or any part of any other city or other in
corporated area located in the same county, pursuant to a written
agreement between the district and such other city or incorpor
ated area approved by their respective governing boards or
bodies. All such agreements referred to in this section shall
be in appropriate form for recording and shall be filed of
record with the county clerk as other instruments relating to
transfer of or creating a lien upon real estate. Any such
agreements entered into by the district pursuant to this section
may provide that the corporate boundaries of the district shall
be enlarged and extended so as to include the real property
of such other city or incorporated area, or part thereof, to be
served pursuant to such agreement, and in such case when
such instrument has been filed of record with the county clerk
as aforesaid, the corporate boundaries of the district shall be
thereby officially enlarged and extended so as to include same.
Such agreements, however, are not valid until approved by the
board of the district, if there be one, created under Section 1
to 17 of this 1962 Act.
(2) The words "incorporated area" as used in this section
shall not mean or apply to any sanitation district organized under
Chapter 220 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes.
Section 23. Section 76.172 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
( 1 ) The ordinance providing for the construction of sewer
age or drainage facilities and appurtenances shall describe the
nature and kind of facilities to be furnished and shall describe
the particular area benefited by said sewerage or drainage fa
cilities.
(2) The costs of the sanitary sewers, combined sewers,
drains, and appurtenances shall be assessed against the land
in said benefited area according to the number of square feet in
any lot or tract within the area described in the ordinance, but
on any sewer or drain greater in size than ten inches in diameter,

Chapter 286 1001
the percentage of cost to be paid by the owners of property in
said assessment area shall not be greater than the estimated
cost of a sewer ten inches in diameter on the same slope, at the
same depth, and of the same materials as the sewer or drain
constructed. The rate of apportionment shall be the same for
each square foot of land in said benefited area, and shall be de
termined by dividing the cost of the assessable sanitary sewers,
combined sewers, drains and appurtenances by the total area of
all land benefited in the area.
(3) The costs of property service connections from the
sewer to the property line or easement line as required shall be
assessed against the individual lots or tracts to which such prop
erty service connections are furnished. The costs to be assessed
for the property service connections shall be fixed by regulation
of the metropolitan sewer district based on its experience of costs
for such work.
(4) All land included in said described territory shall be
assessed, except such property dedicated to use for public
roadways and property owned by cities of the first or second
class, counties containing cities of the first or second class, and
any joint agencies of such cities and counties.
(5) When the board of a metropolitan sewer district deter
mines that such construction of sanitary sewers, combined sewers,
drains, appurtenances or property service connections at the cost
of the property owners shall be recommended to the board of
aldermen of a city of the first class, the metropolitan sewer dis
trict shall cause its engineering department to prepare complete
drawings and specifications for the work and to keep same
available for inspection in its offices.
(6) (a) The actual construction work of the sanitary
sewers, combined sewers, drains, appurtenances or property
service connections constructed pursuant to such ordinance shall
be done by, or under the control of, the metropolitan sewer
district.
(b) The cost of the sanitary sewers, combined sewers,
drains, appurtenances or property service connections shall in
clude not only the actual construction costs and the costs of any
easements required for the sewers, but also costs of surveys,

1002 Acts of the General Assembly
designs, plans, specifications, advertising, inspection and admin
istration; however, these costs other than actual construction
costs and costs of easements shall not exceed fifteen percent of
the actual construction costs of the project. The costs of surveys,
designs, plans, specifications, advertising, inspection and admin
istration, but not exceeding a total of fifteen percent of the actual
construction costs and the cost of any easements shall be paid by
the contractor to the metropolitan sewer district at the comple
tion of the work so that such costs may be included in the appor
tionment warrants.
(7) A lien superior to all liens except the liens for state,
county, city, school and road taxes and hens prior in time for
other public improvements shall exist against the respective lots
or tracts of land for the cost of the sanitary sewers, combined
sewers, drains, appurtenances or property service connections for
apportionment as hereinafter provided for, and interest thereon
at the rate of six percent per annum.
( 8 ) No error in the proceedings of the city legislative body
shall exempt such property from payment after the work has been
done as required by either the ordinance or contract, but the city
legislative body, or the courts in which suits shall be proceeding,
shall make all corrections, rules and orders to do justice to all
parties concerned. In no event, if the sanitary sewers, combined
sewers, drains, appurtenances or property service connections
are constructed as provided, by ordinance or contract, shall the
city or the metropolitan sewer district be liable for the costs of
the sanitary sewers, combined sewers, drains, appurtenances or
property service connections without the right to enforce such
costs against the property receiving the benefit.
(9) Upon completion and acceptance of the sewer facility
constructed, the metropolitan sewer district shall make out all
apportionment warrants for which liens are given for improve
ments of sewer facilities and shall immediately enter them in
alphabetical order upon a register kept for that purpose. When
the holder of the warrant has obtained payment, he shall notify
the metropolitan sewer district and it shall mark upon the register
the fact of payment.
( 10) The lien shall exist from the date of the apportionment

Chapter 286 1003
warrant, but a lien shall not be valid against a purchaser for a
valuable consideration without notice, unless the apportionment
warrant is entered and registered within ten days of its issuance.
(11) After any sewer facilities have been constructed in
conformity with this section the metropolitan sewer district shall
give notice by one publication in a newspaper of general circula
tion in the city of the costs apportioned, and the amounts assessed
and levied on the various tracts of land liable for the payment.
(12) When property is annexed to a city of the first or second
class and subsequently is connected to a sewer owned or operated
by the metropolitan sewer district, payment shall be made to the
district of a proportionate part of the construction costs of the
sewer on the basis that would apply if the sewer were being built
within the corporate limits of the city by apportionment of costs
against the benefited area as provided in this section.
Section 24. Section 76.180 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
The district created under the provisions of this chapter shall
have power to make and promulgate all rules and regulations
proper or necessary to regulate the use, operation, and mainten
ance of property and facilities under its control, and to carry into
effect the powers granted the district by this chapter. The juris
diction of the district shall extend beyond the corporate boundaries
thereof for regulation of any sewers, drains, or water courses,
which by natural flow, or otherwise, empty into or flow through
any part of the district or the district facilities, or are used by the
district or flow through or are constructed in the county in which
such district exists. Provided, however, that such jurisdiction be
yond the corporate boundaries of the district shall cease upon the
organization of a sanitation district pursuant to sections 1 to 17
of this 1962 Act.
Section 25. Section 76.091 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is repealed.

1004 Acts of the General Assembly
CHAPTER 287
(H. B. 493)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Calvert City, in
Marshall County.
WHEREAS, satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Calvert City, in Marshall
County, is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the
fifth class,
NOW, THEREFORE,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
The City of Calvert City, in Marshall County, is transferred
from the fifth to the fourth class of cities.
Approved March 22, 1962
CHAPTER 288
(H. B. 501)
AN ACT relating to traffic regulations.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky:
Section 1. Section 189.340 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
(1) Vehicles overtaking other vehicles proceeding in the
same direction shall pass to the left of them and shall not again
drive to the right until reasonably clear of those vehicles. The
person operating or in charge of the overtaking vehicle shall
sound his horn or other sound device before passing. Vehicles
overtaking street cars may pass either to the right or left when so
directed by a police officer, when on a one-way street or where
the location of the tracks prevents compliance with this section,
with regard for other traffic.
( 2 ) The operator of a vehicle may overtake and pass upon
the right of another vehicle which is making or is about to make

Chapter 288 1005
a left turn. The operator of a vehicle may overtake, and, allowing
sufficient clearance, pass another vehicle proceeding in the same
direction upon the left or upon the right on a roadway with
unobstructed pavement or sufficient width for four or more lines
of traffic when the movement can be made in safety. No person
shall drive off the pavement or upon the shoulder of the roadway
in overtaking or passing on the right.
( 3 ) No vehicle shall be driven to the left side of the center
of the roadway in overtaking and passing another vehicle pro
ceeding in the same direction unless the left side is clearly visible
and free of oncoming traffic for a sufficient distance ahead to
permit overtaking and passing to be completely made without
interfering with the safe operation of any vehicle approaching
from the opposite direction or any vehicle overtaken. In every
event the overtaking vehicle must return to the right-hand side
of the roadway before coming within one hundred feet of any
vehicle approaching from the opposite direction.
[(4) No vehicle shall at any time be driven to the left side
of the roadway under the following conditions:
(a) When approaching the crest of a grade or a curve in
the highway where the driver's view along the highway is
obstructed;
(b) When approaching within one hundred feet of any
bridge, viaduct or tunnel, or when coming within one hundred
feet of or traversing any intersection or railroad grade crossing.
The provisions of this subsection shall not apply upon a one
way roadway.]
(4) [(5)] The Commissioner of Highways is hereby au
thorized to determine those portions of any highway where over
taking and passing or driving to the left of the roadway would
be especially hazardous and may by appropriate signs or markings
on the roadway indicate the beginning and end of such zones,
and when such signs or markings are in place and clearly visible
to an ordinarily observant person, every driver of a vehicle shall
obey the directions thereof.
(5) [(6)] Whenever any roadway has been divided into
three or more clearly marked lanes for traffic the following addi
tional rules shall apply:

tOM Acts of the General Assembly
( a ) A vehicle shall be driven as nearly as may be practical
entirely within a single lane and shall not be moved from that
lane until the driver has first ascertained that the movement can
be made with safety;
(b) A vehicle shall not be driven in the center lane except
when overtaking and passing another vehiele where the roadway
is clearly visible and the center lane is clear of traffic within a
safe distance, or in preparation for a left turn or where a center
lane is at the time allocated exclusively to traffic moving in the
direction in which the vehicle is proceeding and is signposted to
give notice of the allocation;
(c) Official signs may be erected directing slow-moving
traffic to use a designated lane or allocating specified lanes to
traffic moving in the same direction and operators of vehicles shall
obey the directions of such signs.
(8) [(7)] (a) The operator of a motor vehicle shall not
follow another vehicle more closely than is reasonable and
prudent, having regard for the speed of the vehicle and the traffic
upon and condition of the highways.
(b) The operator of any motor truck, semitrailer truck, bus
or heavy construction equipment unit when traveling upon a
highway outside of a business or residential district shall not fol
low within 250 feet of another such vehicle or equipment unit.
This subsection shall not prevent overtaking and passing, nor
shall it apply to any lane specially designated for use of motor
trucks or semitrailer trucks, busses or heavy construction equip
ment units.
Section 2. Section 189.450 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows:
( 1 ) No person shall stop a vehicle, leave it standing or cause

Chapteb 288 1007
extent that it is impossible to avoid the occupation of the main

or emergency vehicles;
(b) Motor vehicles when required to stop in obedience to
the provisions of any section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
or any traffic ordinance, regulation or sign or the command of
any peace officer;
(c) Vehicles operating as common carriers of passengers
for hire and school busses taking passengers on such vehicles or
discharging passengers therefrom, provided that no such vehicles
shall [not] stop for such purposes in a no-passing area as
described in KRS 189.340 (5). [at any point within one hundred
and fifty feet from the brow of a hill or within one hundred and
fifty feet from a curve or when a view of any vehicle approaching
from either direction is obstructed for a distance of one hundred
and fifty feet] ;
( d ) Any vehicle required to stop by reason of an obstruction
to its progress.
(2) Whenever any police officer finds a vehicle standing
upon such a highway in violation of this section, he may move
the vehicle, or require the operator or other person in charge of
the vehicle to move
it,to
aposition off the paved or improved
or main traveled part of the highway.
(3) Whenever any police officer finds
avehicle unattended
upon any bridge or causeway or in
atunnel where the vehicle
constitutes an obstruction to traffic, the officer may provide for
the removal of the vehicle to the nearest garage or other place
of safety.
(4) No person shall stop or park
a vehicle except when
necessary to avoid conflict with other traffic or in compliance
with the directions of
apolice officer or traffic control device, in
the following places:
(a) On
a sidewalk;
(b) In front of
apublic or private driveway;
(c) Within an intersection;

1008 Acts of the General Assembly
(d) At any place where official signs prohibit stopping; or
(e) Within thirty feet upon the approach to any flashing
beacon, stop sign or traffic control signal located at the side of
a roadway.
(5) No person shall move a vehicle not lawfully under his
control into any such prohibited area.
